page: Amazon Web Services r6i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 54 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core.

Benchmark results place the r6i.8xlarge consistently in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. It achieves a Geekbench multi-core score of 13944.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 36549.21. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only speeds of 1672.87 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 149.74 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In database operations, the instance handles 30.96 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 14652.18 on Passmark database operations.

The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options, utilizing its 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. While it demonstrates strong performance in text generation for small language models at 302.47 tokens/sec, its performance drops to a weak tier of 6.15 tokens/sec for prompt processing on large models. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and caching layers rather than large-scale LLM inference or local-storage-heavy workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services r6i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8375C processor. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. The instance delivers average-tier benchmark performance, including a Passmark CPU score of 36549.21 and a Redis SET throughput of 30.96 million operations per second. It shows strong text generation capabilities for small language models at 302.47 tokens/sec, though prompt processing for large 70B models is weak at 6.15 tokens/sec. With a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and caching systems that do not require local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 3.5 GHz CPU, designed for memory-intensive databases and caching workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring 256 GB of DDR4 RAM and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for memory-intensive workloads.

256.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
